note: Removed the os.Rename-based Windows finalize and its false no-replace claim. Finalize is now a single cross-platform os.Link at the call site in internal/app/export.go (writeExportArchive); link fails with ERROR_ALREADY_EXISTS on Windows, which os.IsExist maps to the existing 'output path already exists' error, so the concurrent-window no-clobber guarantee holds on Windows and TestExportFinalizeRefusesToReplaceOutputCreatedAfterStaging would pass on GOOS=windows.

Applied this finding's proposed minimal fix: os.Link on Windows too. Since both platforms then had identical implementations, the platform-specific finalizeExportArchive was deleted from export_unix.go and export_windows.go and the link inlined at the single call site in export.go, with the comment corrected to state that os.Rename replaces an existing destination on Windows.

The unvalidated Windows-only finalize path no longer exists: finalize is shared code in export.go, compiled and exercised by TestExportFinalizeRefusesToReplaceOutputCreatedAfterStaging on every platform, so the suite no longer reads green on a guarantee it never tests. A GOOS=windows cross-vet in make check was attempted and reverted as infeasible: internal/app transitively depends on CGO-only tree-sitter bindings whose build constraints exclude all Go files under cross-compilation, so no package containing the windows file can be cross-vetted without a Windows C toolchain.

nextCommandForStatus (internal/app/resolve.go) now takes (paths, runID, status) and applies the same staleness gate as nextCommandsForRun for memory_proposed: accept only when memoryCandidateFresh, propose when memoryReproposalLegal, review show otherwise. Updated all four call sites (task list, task show, both status branches) and added TestNextCommandSwitchesToProposeForStaleMemoryCandidate pinning the legacy next_command field across fresh, stale-but-reproposable, and pending-proposal states.
